title: Saudiâ€“Spanish JV to Build Green Hydrogen Electrode Plant at SPARK
content:
Saudi Arabiaâ€™s green hydrogen sector is set for a major boost with the development of a new electrode manufacturing facility at King Salman Energy Park. Jolt Green Chemical Industries, a joint venture between a Saudi consortium and Spain's Jolt Solutions, has signed an agreement to build the plant, which will be engineered by Dyar Al-Safwah Engineering Consultancy.

This initiative is a direct response to Saudi Arabiaâ€™s Vision 2030, prioritizing the development of green hydrogen, technology transfer, and local content to achieve 75 percent localization in the energy sector. The project reinforces the Kingdomâ€™s strategic goal of becoming a regional hub for clean energy.

Scheduled to begin operations in the second quarter of 2027, the plant will feature advanced production lines and R&D labs. At full capacity, it will supply over 750,000 square meters of high-performance catalyst-coated electrodes annually. While focusing on green hydrogen, the facility will also serve sectors like petrochemicals, eFuels, batteries, and water desalination. According to the joint venture's CEO, the plant will empower national talent and serve as a platform for technology localization, a direct result of strong government support from the Ministry of Energy.
author: Analysts Account
